response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep web, backend, and systems experience, but does not match this role's core requirements. C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project — far short of 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (MongoDB/MySQL instead), and no fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain background beyond a brief fintech role at RIDE Capital that was Node/TypeScript-based.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured engineering career with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Excellent communication skills, evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015), versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed-income or capital-markets domain expertise; fintech exposure was brief and in a different stack

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
